text:
While recent large language models (LLMs) are able to perform a wide range of novel tasks upon being instructed in natural language or shown a few examples, their development is currently a resource-intensive effort carried out by large corporations. This means that the results are not available to the public in fully open models. Our goal is to develop an open-access alternative, available for everyone, and to ensure that such powerful models are not the exclusive property of a handful of companies. We introduce BLOOM, a new, open-access 176B-parameter language model, developed by a unique, large-scale, and open coalition of over 300 researchers from more than 30 countries.

Open Access BLOOM: a large language model with a focus on multitask learning and unparalleled diversity BLOOM is a decoder-only Transformer model pre-trained on the ROOTS corpus (Gururath et al. 2022) which is a multilingual, multificure corpus in 46 natural and 13 programming languages from 59 different sources. BLOOM is compared to a variety of baseline models demonstrating competitive performance on a range of evaluation sets with even greater gains when doing multitask finetuning using a set of prompts. In order to advance transparent research and to support global development in the use of large language models, the BLOOM model weights and code are available under the Resaonable AI License.
